metadata.career_relevance: Every production-grade Java project uses CI/CD. 'I manually deploy' is a red flag in any interview. GitHub Actions is the most common CI/CD tool for new projects. After this topic: every git push triggers automated tests, Docker build, registry push, and Kubernetes deployment — no manual steps.

hook.real_world_problem:
Manual deploy process at a small fintech:
```
1. Developer runs mvn package locally (5 min)
2. Builds Docker image (3 min)
3. Pushes to registry manually (2 min)
4. SSH into staging server (2 min)
5. kubectl apply manually (2 min)
Total: 14 minutes, error-prone, requires developer attention

With GitHub Actions:
Developer: git push origin main
→ GitHub Actions starts automatically
→ Tests run (parallel: unit + integration) → 4 min
→ Docker build + push to ECR → 2 min
→ kubectl apply to staging → 1 min
→ Health check verification → 1 min
Total: 8 min, fully automated, no human error
```
hook.what_you_will_build: A GitHub Actions workflow that tests, builds, pushes to Docker Hub, and deploys to Kubernetes on every push to main.

concept_explanation.workflow:
**Complete CI/CD GitHub Actions workflow:**

```yaml
# .github/workflows/cicd.yml
name: Payment Service CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: razorpay/payment-service

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Set up Java 21
      uses: actions/setup-java@v4
      with:
        java-version: '21'
        distribution: 'temurin'
        cache: maven
    - name: Run tests
      run: mvn test --batch-mode
    - name: Upload test results
      if: always()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: test-results
        path: target/surefire-reports/

  build-push:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Log in to GitHub Container Registry
      uses: docker/login-action@v3
      with:
        registry: ghcr.io
        username: ${{ github.actor }}
        password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    - name: Build and push Docker image
      uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
      with:
        push: true
        tags: ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }}
        cache-from: type=gha
        cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build-push
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Deploy to k8s
      run: |
        kubectl set image deployment/payment-service \
          payment-service=ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:${{ github.sha }} \
          --namespace payments
        kubectl rollout status deployment/payment-service -n payments --timeout=5m
      env:
        KUBECONFIG: ${{ secrets.KUBECONFIG_STAGING }}
```

visual_aid.description: GitHub Actions pipeline: git push → GitHub Actions triggers. 3 jobs: test (parallel: unit + integration) → build-push (Docker build → GHCR push) → deploy-staging (kubectl set image → rollout status). Arrows show dependencies (test → build-push → deploy-staging). Secret icons on GHCR login and kubectl steps. GitHub Environment 'staging' shown as approval gate.
visual_aid.alt_text: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with test, build-push, and deploy stages

worked_example.scenario: Full CI/CD for Razorpay payment service: test → build → push to GHCR → deploy to k8s.
worked_example.problem_statement: Write GitHub Actions workflow. Explain: GITHUB_TOKEN, SHA-based image tags, environment protection.

step_by_step_solution.details: Image tag = git commit SHA (e.g., :abc1234f). This makes every image immutable and traceable: see an image in production → know exactly which commit it contains. Never use :latest in production — :latest is mutable, makes rollbacks impossible.
environment: staging creates a protection gate. Can require manual approval before deploy. Has its own secrets (KUBECONFIG_STAGING vs KUBECONFIG_PROD). Audit trail: who approved what deployment.

worked_example.final_code:
# Trigger: git push origin main
# Actions: test → Docker build → push GHCR → kubectl deploy → rollout verify
worked_example.expected_output:
✓ Run tests (2m 34s)
✓ Build and push Docker image (1m 12s)
✓ Deploy to k8s (1m 06s)
All checks passed! Deployed: ghcr.io/razorpay/payment-service:abc1234f
things_to_notice: cache: maven in setup-java: downloads Maven deps once and caches them. Without this: every run re-downloads 150MB of dependencies (1-2 minutes wasted). With cache: cache hit saves ~90s per run.
cache-from: type=gha in build-push-action: caches Docker layers in GitHub Actions cache. The 'dependencies' layer (rarely changes) is reused across runs. Saves 60+ seconds per build.
if: always() on upload-artifact: uploads test results even when tests fail. Without this: you lose test reports on the most important runs (the failing ones).
github.sha for image tag: every commit = unique immutable image. If a deployment causes issues: rollback = change image tag back to previous SHA. Deterministic and auditable.
needs: test: jobs run in parallel by default; needs: creates explicit dependency chain. test must pass before build-push starts.

industry_examples.use_case: ~50 merges/day to main. Each merge triggers: 800 unit tests + 200 integration tests (parallel) → Docker build → push to ECR → deploy to staging → automatic smoke tests → if all pass → deploy to production. Engineers never manually deploy.

applications.interview_relevance: 'Describe your CI/CD pipeline.' → GitHub Actions triggered on push to main. Jobs: test (mvn test), build (docker build + push to registry with SHA tag), deploy (kubectl set image → rollout status). Environment gates for staging/prod. Secrets managed via GitHub Secrets.
applications.real_world_workflow: CI/CD maturity: manual deploy → script → GitHub Actions → multi-stage (staging→prod with approval) → automated smoke tests + rollback.

common_gaps.what_students_get_wrong: Tagging Docker images with :latest in CI/CD. Impossible to know which code is running in production.
common_gaps.remediation: Always use immutable tags: git SHA (${{ github.sha }}), version tags (${{ github.ref_name }}), or semantic version. :latest should only exist as an alias that also gets the SHA tag.
common_gaps.detection_pattern: kubectl describe pod shows image: myapp:latest — impossible to determine deployment history

key_takeaways: GitHub Actions: event-driven automation (push → test → build → deploy)
jobs run in parallel; `needs`: creates sequential dependency
`cache: maven` in setup-java: skip re-downloading 150MB deps per run
SHA image tag: immutable, traceable, rollback-friendly
`if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`: only deploy from main branch
GitHub Environments: protection gates + per-environment secrets
`kubectl set image`: targeted image update without full apply
`rollout status --timeout`: CI waits for k8s to confirm successful deploy

self_check_questions.question: Why use SHA-based image tags instead of :latest?
self_check_questions.answer: :latest is mutable — it points to a different image every build. If you need to rollback production, you'd run kubectl rollout undo, which undoes to the previous deployment. But if both deployments used :latest, the previous deployment would pull the same :latest image (the bad one) when pods restart. SHA tags are immutable: abc1234f always refers to exactly that commit's image. Rollback = set image back to the known-good SHA. Incident investigation: see SHA in production → know exact code. Audit: every deployment in history shows which commit was deployed and when.

reflection.next_topic_preview: Final Module 21 topic: Blue-Green & Canary Deployments — shipping to a small percentage of users first, measuring metrics, and progressively rolling out (or instantly rolling back) before all users are affected.
